important_issues.issue: String concatenation for thinking field merging in streaming responses
important_issues.impact: Potential memory issues with large thought streams and inefficient string handling
important_issues.recommendation: Consider using StringBuilder for better performance or implement streaming buffer limits

important_issues.issue: Think field defaults to null with behavior dependent on Ollama version compatibility
important_issues.impact: Could cause unexpected behavior with older Ollama versions that don't support thinking
important_issues.recommendation: Add version checking or graceful degradation for unsupported Ollama versions

risk_factors: No input validation on thinking field content - could store arbitrarily large thought data affecting memory usage
Missing null safety checks in some thinking field handling - potential NullPointerException in edge cases
Thread safety not explicitly addressed for thinking data in concurrent streaming scenarios
No documentation on thinking field size limits or performance implications for large thought streams
positive_findings: Proper use of @JsonProperty annotations for consistent API mapping
Comprehensive test coverage including both positive and negative thinking scenarios
Backward compatibility maintained - existing code without thinking support continues to work
Clean separation of concerns with thinking field isolated to appropriate classes
Proper null handling in merge operations for streaming responses
Integration tests properly validate both thinking enabled and disabled scenarios
risk_summary: The implementation is well-structured with good test coverage and maintains backward compatibility. Main concerns are around performance optimization for large thinking streams and ensuring graceful handling of unsupported Ollama versions.
